attributes.name: Clinical Laboratory Technician
attributes.description: Laboratory professional performing diagnostic tests on blood, tissue, and body fluids.

attributes.name: Clinical Trial Coordinator
attributes.description: Research professional managing trial logistics, regulatory compliance, and participant safety.

attributes.name: Pharmacist
attributes.description: Licensed medication expert dispensing prescriptions, counseling patients, and monitoring therapy.

attributes.name: Surgeon
attributes.description: Physician trained in operative techniques to treat disease, injury, or deformity.

attributes.name: Registered Nurse (RN)
attributes.description: Licensed nurse providing direct patient care, medication administration, and patient education.

attributes.name: Mental Health Provider
attributes.description: Psychiatrist, psychologist, or licensed counselor diagnosing and treating mental health conditions.

attributes.name: Insurance Underwriter
attributes.description: Professional assessing risk and setting premium rates for health insurance policies.

attributes.name: Regulatory Affairs Specialist
attributes.description: Professional ensuring compliance with FDA, CMS, and international health regulations.

attributes.name: Specialist Physician
attributes.description: Board-certified physician focused on a specific organ system, disease, or patient population.

attributes.name: Hospital CEO / System Executive
attributes.description: Senior executive responsible for strategic direction, financial performance, and organizational culture.

attributes.name: Anesthesiologist
attributes.description: Physician specializing in perioperative anesthesia, pain management, and critical care.

attributes.name: Radiologist
attributes.description: Physician interpreting medical imaging and performing image-guided interventions.

attributes.name: Nurse Practitioner (NP)
attributes.description: Advanced practice nurse with prescriptive authority providing primary and specialty care.

attributes.name: Pediatrician
attributes.description: Physician providing medical care for infants, children, and adolescents.

attributes.name: Primary Care Physician
attributes.description: Generalist physician providing preventive, acute, and chronic care coordination.

attributes.name: Emergency Medicine Physician
attributes.description: Physician specializing in acute care, trauma, and stabilization in emergency departments.

attributes.name: Health Information Manager
attributes.description: Professional managing patient records, health data integrity, privacy, and informatics workflows.

attributes.name: Physical Therapist
attributes.description: Rehabilitation professional restoring mobility, function, and quality of life after injury or illness.

attributes.name: Geriatrician
attributes.description: Physician specializing in care for older adults, including polypharmacy and functional decline.

attributes.name: Claims Adjuster
attributes.specialty: medical claims, fraud detection, utilization review
attributes.description: Insurance professional reviewing, investigating, and settling healthcare claims.

attributes.name: Disease Surveillance System
attributes.description: Systems for detecting, reporting, and analyzing disease outbreaks and health trends at population scale.

attributes.name: General Inpatient Wards
attributes.description: Standard medical-surgical floors for non-critical admissions.

attributes.name: Health Analytics & AI
attributes.description: Population health analytics, predictive models, clinical AI, and real-world evidence platforms.

attributes.name: CDRH
attributes.description: Center for Devices and Radiological Health.

attributes.description: Federal cabinet department overseeing health, welfare, and public health agencies including CMS, CDC, FDA, NIH, and HRSA.

attributes.name: Intensive Care Units

attributes.name: Medical Devices & Diagnostics

attributes.description: In-vitro and in-vivo safety and pharmacology studies.

attributes.name: Mail-Order Pharmacy

attributes.description: Beds, staff, and supplies available for sudden demand spikes.

attributes.description: Federal, state, and local medical response teams for mass-casualty events.

attributes.description: Vaccination programs, screenings, behavioral interventions, and health education campaigns.

attributes.description: Operating rooms, perioperative care, anesthesia, and same-day surgery.

attributes.description: Condition-specific care: cardiology, oncology, orthopedics, mental health, and other subspecialties.

attributes.description: Regional and national networks enabling interoperable clinical data exchange between disparate systems.

attributes.name: Home Health Agencies
attributes.description: Nursing, therapy, and aide services delivered in patient homes.

attributes.name: Pharma & Life Sciences
attributes.description: Research, development, manufacturing, and distribution of drugs, biologics, and medical devices.

attributes.name: Store & Manage
attributes.description: HIPAA-compliant storage, backup, and retention.

attributes.name: Execute Clinical Trials
attributes.description: GCP-compliant Phase I-III trial operations.

attributes.name: Pool Financial Risk
attributes.description: Aggregate enrollee premiums and tax revenues to fund healthcare expenditures across populations.

attributes.name: Triage Patients
attributes.description: Rapid assessment and prioritization of incoming patients.

attributes.name: Identify Disparities
attributes.description: Geographic and demographic health equity analysis.

attributes.name: Process Claims & Payment
attributes.description: Verify eligibility, adjudicate claims, apply prior authorization, and disburse reimbursement.

attributes.name: Coordinate Care Transitions
attributes.description: Discharge planning, referrals, and handoffs.

attributes.name: Ensure Quality & Safety
attributes.description: Inspect facilities, accredit providers, monitor adverse events, and enforce clinical standards.

attributes.name: Verify Eligibility
attributes.description: Real-time insurance eligibility and benefit verification.

attributes.name: Capture Data
attributes.description: Structured and unstructured clinical data acquisition at point of care.

attributes.name: Regulate Products & Services
attributes.description: Evaluate safety, efficacy, and manufacturing quality of drugs, devices, and clinical services.

attributes.name: Exchange Health Information
attributes.description: Enable secure, interoperable flow of clinical, administrative, and financial data across stakeholders.

attributes.name: Discover Targets
attributes.description: Biological target identification and validation.

attributes.name: Disburse Payment
attributes.description: Remittance generation, EFT, and patient balance billing.

attributes.name: Run Preclinical Studies
attributes.description: GLP toxicology and pharmacokinetic evaluation.

attributes.name: Deliver Clinical Care
attributes.description: Provide diagnosis, treatment, surgery, rehabilitation, and preventive services to patients.

attributes.name: Educate Health Workforce
attributes.description: Train, credential, and continuously certify physicians, nurses, allied health, and administrators.

attributes.name: Diagnose Conditions
attributes.description: Clinical reasoning, testing, and differential diagnosis.

attributes.name: Set Health Policy
attributes.description: Establish laws, regulations, and guidelines governing coverage, pricing, quality, and access.

attributes.name: Develop Therapies
attributes.description: Discover, test, manufacture, and bring new drugs, biologics, and devices to market.

attributes.name: Transmit Securely
attributes.description: Encrypted point-to-point and network-based data exchange.

attributes.name: Detect Outbreaks
attributes.description: Statistical anomaly detection and cluster identification.

attributes.name: Monitor Population Health
attributes.description: Track disease burden, health disparities, and environmental health risks across geographies and demographics.

attributes.name: Monitor Adverse Events
attributes.description: Post-market surveillance and signal detection.

attributes.name: Adjudicate Claims
attributes.description: Medical necessity review, pricing, and denial management.

attributes.name: Track Disease Burden
attributes.description: Incidence, prevalence, and mortality monitoring.

attributes.name: Accredit Providers
attributes.description: Standards review, survey, and accreditation decision.

attributes.name: Treat & Manage
attributes.description: Therapeutic intervention, medication, surgery, and therapy.

attributes.name: Inspect Facilities
attributes.description: Physical inspection and environmental-of-care surveys.

attributes.name: Analyze & Report
attributes.description: Analytics, visualization, and population-level reporting.

attributes.name: Submit to Regulators
attributes.description: NDA, BLA, and 510(k) compilation and filing.

attributes.name: Population Health Data Interface
attributes.description: Aggregated, de-identified data exchange for disease surveillance, epidemiology, and population risk scoring.

attributes.name: Clinical Laboratory Integration Interface
attributes.description: Bi-directional interface for orders, results, and specimen tracking between providers and laboratories.

attributes.name: Clinical-Financial Data Exchange
attributes.description: Primary interface between care delivery and health financing for claims, eligibility, and remittance.

attributes.name: Payer-Provider Collaboration Interface
attributes.protocol: FHIR Prior Auth / X12 278 / Real-Time Adjudication
attributes.description: Workflow interface for prior authorization, referral management, and value-based care contracts.

attributes.name: Pharma Supply Chain Interface
attributes.description: Tracks drug provenance, serialization, and distribution from manufacturer to patient.

attributes.name: Patient Access & Engagement Interface
attributes.protocol: FHIR Patient Access API / SMART on FHIR
attributes.description: Consumer-facing interface enabling patients to access records, schedule visits, and share data with apps.

attributes.name: Regulatory Mandate Interface
attributes.protocol: Federal Register / CMS Rules / FDA Guidance
attributes.description: Channel through which laws, regulations, accreditation standards, and enforcement actions flow to ecosystem actors.

attributes.name: Health IT Interoperability Interface
attributes.description: Standardized clinical data exchange across EHR vendors, HIE networks, and telehealth platforms.

attributes.name: Claims Input Port
attributes.description: Channel receiving claim transactions from care delivery providers.

attributes.name: Premium Input Port
attributes.description: Channel receiving premium revenue from enrollees and employers.

attributes.name: Safety Signal Input Port
attributes.description: Channel receiving adverse event and post-market surveillance reports.

attributes.name: Market Entry Port
attributes.description: Channel authorizing approved drugs and devices to enter commercial distribution.

attributes.name: Clinical Data Output Port
attributes.description: Channel emitting de-identified clinical data for surveillance and quality reporting.

attributes.name: Policy Output Port
attributes.description: Channel through which regulations, mandates, and guidance are emitted.

attributes.name: Payment Output Port
attributes.description: Channel disbursing reimbursement to providers and pharmacies.

attributes.name: Clinical Data Input Port
attributes.description: Channel receiving patient records, orders, and results from EHR systems.

attributes.name: Hospital Compare Reporting
attributes.description: Public reporting of process, outcome, and patient experience measures.

attributes.name: Prior Authorization Limitations
attributes.description: Evidence-based prior auth criteria and rapid turnaround requirements.

attributes.name: Medicare Star Ratings
attributes.description: Part C and Part D quality measure aggregation and publication.

attributes.name: Network Adequacy Standards
attributes.description: Timely access to in-network primary and specialty care.

attributes.name: Health Equity Goals
attributes.description: Disparities in outcomes, access, and quality across racial, ethnic, and socioeconomic groups must be reduced.

attributes.name: Quality Reporting Requirements
attributes.description: Providers and payers must report standardized clinical quality measures and outcomes to regulators.

attributes.name: FDA Safety & Efficacy Standards
attributes.description: All marketed drugs and devices must demonstrate adequate safety and efficacy through controlled trials.

attributes.name: Interoperability Standards
attributes.description: EHR systems must support standardized APIs and data formats for seamless health information exchange.

attributes.name: HIPAA Administrative Safeguards
attributes.description: Security management process, assigned security responsibility, workforce training.

attributes.name: FHIR API Compliance
attributes.description: Standardized FHIR R4 RESTful APIs for patient access and provider exchange.

attributes.name: Cost Containment Targets
attributes.description: Annual healthcare expenditure growth must not exceed GDP growth plus a defined margin.

attributes.name: Phase I Safety Profile
attributes.description: Initial human safety, tolerability, and pharmacokinetic characterization.

attributes.name: Phase III Efficacy Evidence
attributes.description: Controlled trials demonstrating efficacy in the intended population.

attributes.name: Value-Based Payment Metrics
attributes.description: Quality and cost measure bundles for ACO and bundled payment programs.

attributes.name: HIPAA Physical Safeguards
attributes.description: Facility access controls, workstation security, device and media controls.

attributes.name: Universal Access Mandate
attributes.description: All residents must have timely access to essential health services without financial hardship.

attributes.name: USCDI Data Class Support
attributes.description: Support for all required U.S. Core Data for Interoperability classes.

attributes.name: Essential Health Benefits
attributes.description: Coverage of ten essential benefit categories without annual/lifetime limits.

attributes.name: HIPAA Technical Safeguards
attributes.description: Access control, audit controls, integrity, transmission security.

attributes.name: Preclinical Safety Data
attributes.description: Adequate pharmacology and toxicology studies before human exposure.

attributes.name: HIPAA Privacy & Security
attributes.description: Protected health information must be safeguarded with administrative, physical, and technical controls.

attributes.name: Patient Access API
attributes.description: Enable patients to access their data via third-party applications.

attributes.name: Laboratory Results
attributes.description: Structured lab results with LOINC codes flowing to EHRs and patient portals.

attributes.name: Clinical Trial Data
attributes.description: Study protocols, results, and safety updates submitted from trial sites to FDA and public registries.

attributes.name: Formulary & Rebate Updates
attributes.description: PBMs communicate covered drug lists, tier placements, and negotiated rebate terms to payers and providers.

attributes.name: Legislative Mandates
attributes.description: New laws and policy changes (e.g., ACA updates, drug pricing reform) broadcast to all ecosystem actors.

attributes.name: EHR Data Exchange
attributes.description: HL7 FHIR-based clinical summaries, care documents, and real-time notifications between provider systems.

attributes.name: Reimbursement Rate Updates
attributes.description: CMS publishes annual fee schedules, DRG weights, and value-based payment model benchmarks.

attributes.name: Adverse Event Reports
attributes.description: Mandatory and voluntary safety reports from providers and manufacturers to FDA MedWatch.

attributes.name: Public Health Alerts
attributes.description: CDC and state health departments broadcast outbreak notices, recalls, and vaccination guidance.

attributes.name: Drug & Device Approvals
attributes.description: FDA approval signals enabling market entry; includes labeling and REMS requirements.

attributes.name: Claims Submissions
attributes.description: Standardized claim transactions (837) sent from providers to payers for services rendered.

attributes.name: Clinical Quality Measures
attributes.description: Standardized outcome and process metrics reported from providers and plans to CMS and accreditors.

attributes.name: Premium Collections
attributes.description: Monthly/quarterly premium payments flowing from employers and individuals to insurance plans.

attributes.name: Epidemiological Surveillance Data
attributes.description: De-identified case reports, lab results, and syndromic data flowing to CDC and state health departments.

attributes.name: Reimbursement Payments
attributes.description: Remittance advice and payment (835/EFT) from payers back to providers and pharmacies.

attributes.name: Prescription Fill Status
attributes.description: Real-time prescription fill and pickup notifications from pharmacies to providers.

attributes.name: Clinical Documentation
attributes.description: Progress notes, discharge summaries, and consult notes exchanged across the care continuum.

attributes.name: Prior Authorization Requests
attributes.description: Electronic prior authorization requests and determinations between providers and payers.

attributes.name: Hospital Administrators
attributes.description: Executives and managers overseeing hospital operations, finance, strategy, and compliance.

attributes.name: Labcorp
attributes.description: Major US clinical laboratory and drug development services company (formerly Laboratory Corporation of America).

attributes.name: Patient Advocacy Groups
attributes.concerns: patient rights, access to care, health equity
attributes.description: Nonprofits and community organizations representing patient interests, rights, and access.

attributes.description: Integrated managed-care consortium combining insurance (Kaiser Foundation Health Plan) with care delivery (Permanente Medical Groups).

attributes.name: Oscar Health
attributes.description: Technology-focused health insurer selling individual, Medicare Advantage, and small-group plans.

attributes.name: CommonSpirit Health
attributes.description: Largest Catholic nonprofit health system by revenue, operating 140+ hospitals across 24 states.

attributes.name: Intermountain Health
attributes.description: Utah-based nonprofit system operating 33 hospitals; pioneering value-based care and generic drug manufacturing (Civica Rx).

attributes.name: Employer Sponsors

attributes.description: Largest US EHR vendor by hospital market share (~35%); known for MyChart patient portal and Care Everywhere interoperability.

attributes.name: Amwell
attributes.description: Enterprise telehealth platform powering virtual care for health systems, payers, and employers.

attributes.name: Community Health Systems

attributes.description: Largest for-profit hospital operator in the US with ~180 hospitals and ~2,400 ambulatory sites.

attributes.name: Purchaser Business Group on Health (PBGH)

attributes.description: Diversified medtech with diagnostics, nutrition, established pharma, and medical devices (FreeStyle Libre).

attributes.name: Aetna (CVS Health)

attributes.name: Trinity Health
attributes.description: Major Catholic nonprofit system operating 90+ hospitals in 25 states, focused on community health.

attributes.name: Clinical Laboratories

attributes.name: Cigna Corporation
attributes.description: Global health services organization offering commercial insurance, Medicare, international coverage, and Express Scripts PBM.

attributes.name: Teladoc Health

attributes.name: Johnson & Johnson
attributes.description: World's largest diversified healthcare company spanning pharmaceuticals (Janssen), medical devices, and consumer health.

attributes.name: Molina Healthcare

attributes.name: Mayo Clinic

attributes.name: athenahealth

attributes.name: Sutter Health

attributes.name: Government Regulators
attributes.concerns: public safety, market integrity, health equity
attributes.description: Federal and state agencies that set rules, enforce standards, and protect public health.

attributes.name: American Nurses Association (ANA)
attributes.description: Premier organization representing the interests of 4+ million registered nurses in the United States.

attributes.name: Physician Workforce
attributes.description: Licensed medical doctors who diagnose, treat, and manage patient care across specialties.

attributes.name: AARP Health Advocacy
attributes.description: Major consumer advocacy organization representing 38+ million older Americans on Medicare, drug pricing, and long-term care.

attributes.name: Devoted Health
attributes.description: Medicare Advantage startup focused on seniors, combining insurance with virtual and in-home care.

attributes.name: AmerisourceBergen (Cencora)
attributes.description: Global pharmaceutical sourcing and distribution company recently rebranded as Cencora.

attributes.name: Humana
attributes.description: Major Medicare Advantage insurer with growing primary-care center investments (CenterWell).

attributes.name: Merck & Co. (MSD)
attributes.description: Major US pharma with leading vaccines, oncology (Keytruda), and animal health divisions.

attributes.name: Vizient
attributes.description: Largest healthcare GPO by purchasing volume, serving 60% of US hospitals with contracting and supply chain analytics.

attributes.name: Elevance Health (Anthem)
attributes.description: Major Blue Cross Blue Shield licensee operating in 14 states; commercial, Medicare, Medicaid plans.

attributes.name: The Joint Commission (TJC)
attributes.description: Independent nonprofit that accredits and certifies 22,000+ US health care organizations and programs; sets quality and safety standards.

attributes.name: Oracle Health (Cerner)
attributes.description: Major EHR vendor acquired by Oracle; strong in federal VA/MHS contracts and revenue cycle.

attributes.name: Dexcom
attributes.description: Leading continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) company for diabetes management.

attributes.name: Dexterity (Health Data Exchange)
attributes.description: Represents health data exchange platforms and interoperability vendors facilitating cross-system data sharing.

attributes.name: Tenet Healthcare
attributes.description: For-profit operator of 60+ hospitals and 550+ outpatient centers; also owns USPI ambulatory surgery.

attributes.name: American Cancer Society
attributes.description: Nationwide community-based voluntary health organization dedicated to eliminating cancer through research, education, and advocacy.

attributes.name: National Committee for Quality Assurance (NCQA)
attributes.description: Private nonprofit that reviews and accredits health plans and medical groups; manages HEDIS quality measures.

attributes.name: Patient Population
attributes.description: Individuals and communities who receive healthcare services, generate health data, and bear outcomes.

attributes.name: eClinicalWorks
attributes.description: EHR and practice management vendor with strong ambulatory and chronic-care management focus.

attributes.name: MEDITECH
attributes.description: Long-standing EHR vendor focused on community hospitals and cloud-based Expanse platform.

attributes.name: Northwell Health
attributes.description: Largest health system in New York State with 21 hospitals and 850+ outpatient facilities.

attributes.name: American Heart Association
attributes.description: Leading voluntary health organization funding cardiovascular research and advocating for heart-healthy policies.

attributes.name: Medtronic
attributes.description: World's largest medical device company; leading in cardiac devices, diabetes, spine, and surgical robotics.

attributes.name: Mass General Brigham
attributes.description: Harvard-affiliated nonprofit system including Massachusetts General Hospital and Brigham and Women's Hospital.

attributes.name: CVS Caremark
attributes.description: One of the 'Big Three' PBMs managing pharmacy benefits for 100+ million lives; owned by CVS Health.

attributes.name: HealthTrust (HCA)
attributes.description: GPO owned by HCA Healthcare serving HCA facilities and 1,600+ other hospitals with supply chain and workforce solutions.

attributes.name: UnitedHealth Group
attributes.description: Largest US health insurer through UnitedHealthcare; also owns Optum (PBM, care delivery, analytics).

attributes.name: Pharmaceutical Companies
attributes.concerns: R&D ROI, patent protection, pricing, FDA approval
attributes.description: Organizations researching, developing, manufacturing, and marketing drugs and biologics.

attributes.name: Johns Hopkins Medicine
attributes.description: Premier academic medical system combining Johns Hopkins Hospital with schools of medicine, nursing, and public health.

attributes.name: NYU Langone Health
attributes.description: Premier academic medical center in New York City with top-ranked research, education, and patient care.

attributes.name: Premier Inc.
attributes.description: Leading GPO and performance improvement alliance serving 4,400+ hospitals and health systems.

attributes.name: Cardinal Health
attributes.description: Major distributor of pharmaceuticals and medical products; also manufactures surgical and infection-prevention products.

attributes.name: Pfizer
attributes.description: Global pharmaceutical leader in vaccines, oncology, and internal medicine; known for mRNA COVID-19 vaccine.

attributes.name: Advocate Health
attributes.description: Large nonprofit system formed from Advocate Aurora Health merger; operates 67 hospitals across Illinois, Wisconsin, North Carolina, and Georgia.

attributes.name: Siemens Healthineers
attributes.description: Global medtech leader in imaging, diagnostics, and advanced therapies; spun from Siemens AG.

attributes.name: Novartis
attributes.description: Swiss multinational pharma with strong oncology, generics (Sandoz), and gene/cell therapy portfolio.

attributes.name: Quest Diagnostics
attributes.description: Largest clinical lab network in the US performing diagnostic testing for hospitals, physicians, and consumers.

attributes.name: Academic Researchers
attributes.description: Universities, medical schools, and research institutes advancing clinical and translational science.

attributes.name: OptumRx (UnitedHealth)
attributes.description: UnitedHealth's PBM serving 65+ million people with pharmacy care services, specialty pharmacy, and home delivery.

attributes.name: Cardiology Data Exchange
attributes.description: Standardized exchange of ECG, echocardiogram, and catheterization data between cardiology systems.

attributes.name: Oncology Interoperability
attributes.description: FHIR-based exchange of cancer registries, genomic markers, and treatment protocols.

attributes.name: Emergency Dispatch Integration
attributes.description: Real-time integration between EMS dispatch, emergency departments, and trauma centers.

attributes.name: ICU Remote Monitoring
attributes.description: Continuous vital signs and alarm telemetry from ICU beds to central monitoring stations.

attributes.name: Inpatient EHR Integration
attributes.description: HL7 FHIR interface connecting inpatient EHR with pharmacy, lab, and radiology systems.

attributes.name: Ambulatory EHR Integration
attributes.description: Integration between ambulatory EHR, patient portals, and referral management.

attributes.name: Telehealth Video Platform
attributes.description: HIPAA-compliant video, audio, and screen-sharing interface for virtual visits.

attributes.name: Wearables Data Sync
attributes.description: Bluetooth / REST API synchronization of consumer and clinical wearable devices.

attributes.name: FDA Regulatory Submission
attributes.description: Electronic submission gateway for IND, NDA, PMA, and 510(k) regulatory filings.

attributes.name: CMS Claims Processing
attributes.description: X12 837/835 electronic claims submission and remittance for Medicare/Medicaid.

attributes.name: Accreditation Survey Exchange
attributes.description: Data exchange platform for Joint Commission, NCQA, and URAC accreditation surveys.

attributes.name: Pharma Manufacturing Execution
attributes.description: MES / ERP integration for batch records, quality control, and serialization.

attributes.name: Supply Chain Track & Trace
attributes.description: GS1-standardized serialization and traceability for drugs and devices.

attributes.name: Disaster Alert Network
attributes.description: Multi-agency alert and resource coordination interface for emergency response.

attributes.name: Surveillance Reporting
attributes.description: Automated case reporting from EHR to public health agencies via eCR.

attributes.name: Immunization Registry Exchange
attributes.description: Bidirectional exchange of vaccination records with state immunization registries.

attributes.name: Home Health Sync
attributes.description: Integration between home health agencies, remote monitoring, and primary care.

attributes.name: Long-Term Care Integration
attributes.description: Data sharing between skilled nursing, hospice, and post-acute care providers.

attributes.name: Mental Health Data Sharing
attributes.description: Secure exchange of behavioral health records with primary care and crisis services.

attributes.name: Clinical Research Data Interface
attributes.description: De-identified data extraction interface for clinical trials and real-world evidence.

attributes.name: ECG Data Port
attributes.description: Output port for electrocardiogram waveform data.

attributes.name: Lab Order Input Port
attributes.description: Input port for laboratory test orders.

attributes.name: Lab Result Output Port
attributes.description: Output port for laboratory results.

attributes.name: Prescription Input Port
attributes.description: Input port for electronic prescriptions.

attributes.name: Prescription Output Port
attributes.description: Output port for prescription fill status.

attributes.name: Imaging Output Port
attributes.description: Output port for DICOM imaging studies.

attributes.name: ADT Input Port
attributes.description: Input port for admission, discharge, and transfer notifications.

attributes.name: Billing Output Port
attributes.description: Output port for charge capture and billing.

attributes.name: Vitals Input Port
attributes.description: Input port for continuous vital signs streams.

attributes.name: Alert Output Port
attributes.description: Output port for critical alarm and alert notifications.

attributes.name: Telehealth Input Port
attributes.description: Input port for video session initiation.

attributes.name: Device Sync Port
attributes.description: Bidirectional port for medical device synchronization.
